steps.title: Spot the interrupting phrase
steps.content: After the blank comes claims dendrochronologist Valerie Trouet — an attribution that interrupts the flow from the subject a single tree like [this] to the main predicate can tell the history of the world.

steps.title: Apply the rule for interrupting phrases
steps.content:
A comma after this sets off the interrupting attribution phrase, keeping the main clause intact: a single tree like this ... can tell the history of the world.

No punctuation fails to set off the phrase; a colon suggests elaboration rather than interruption; a semicolon implies separate independent clauses.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
